heroSubtitle: Browse makes, decode VINs, compare specs side by side.
aiQuestionVin: What should I check in a VIN?
aiQuestionFirstCar: How do I choose a first car?
aiAnswerFlat6: A flat-6 is a six-cylinder engine with opposing banks of cylinders. It keeps the center of gravity low and is known for smooth power delivery, which is why brands like Porsche use it in performance cars.
aiAnswerBestEv: A good EV balances real-world range, charging speed, efficiency, warranty, and cabin practicality. For daily use, charging access and predictable range usually matter more than peak horsepower.
aiAnswerAwdVs4wd: AWD is usually automatic and optimized for road grip in rain, snow, or quick acceleration. 4WD is often more rugged, with low-range gearing for off-road use, towing, or very loose surfaces.
aiAnswerVin: A VIN can confirm the model year, manufacturer, body type, and basic vehicle configuration. Always compare the decoded data with the listing, registration documents, and the VIN stamped on the vehicle.
aiAnswerFirstCar: For a first car, prioritize reliability, insurance cost, service history, safety equipment, and simple maintenance. A less exciting car with clear history is usually a better buy than a bargain with hidden repairs.
aiAnswerHybridVsEv: Choose an EV if you can charge regularly and most trips fit its real-world range. A hybrid is easier when you drive long distances often, lack home charging, or want lower fuel use without changing habits.
aiAnswerTurbo: A turbo uses exhaust energy to push more air into the engine, helping it make more power from a smaller displacement. It can improve performance and efficiency, but maintenance and oil quality matter.
aiAnswerMaintenance: Oil changes, tires, brakes, fluids, battery health, and service records tell you more than a clean exterior. Before buying used, check for overdue maintenance and repeated warning lights.
aiNoMatch: Try asking about engines, EVs, VINs, buying tips, or drivetrain differences.
